data.title: Singlife partners Fullerton to expand investment offerings on GROW
data.description:
GROW will serve as a key wealth platform for Fullerton’s investment solutions.

Singlife and its investment platform GROW has announced a preferred partnership with Fullerton Fund Management.

Singlife will be able to offer its customers and financial advisers' access to Fullerton solutions across ILPs and its investment platforms GROW and dollarDEX. GROW will play a central role in this partnership, serving as a key wealth platform for Fullerton’s investment solutions, according to the press release.

Meanwhile, Fullerton will leverage Singlife’s established distribution network across B2B and D2C channels to offer investment opportunities.

This partnership with Fullerton strengthens Singlife’s unique ability to provide holistic solutions both in insurance and investments, said Helen Shen, group head of products, Singlife.

“By marrying Fullerton’s investment expertise with Singlife’s trusted distribution channels, we are creating powerful synergy that allows us to deliver better outcomes for our customers,” Shen said.

data.title: Gamuda wins $1.01b land tender in Singapore
data.description:
It will comprise up to 875 residential units.

Gamuda Bhd has secured a Housing Development Board (HDB) land parcel in Singapore worth $1.012b (RM3.31b).

The tender, awarded on 26 September and accepted on 29 September, was submitted by Gamuda’s wholly owned subsidiary Gamuda (Singapore) Pte Ltd together with Evia MCS Pte Ltd and H108 Pte Ltd.

The land will be acquired through joint-venture companies Polaris Times Square Residences Pte Ltd and Times Square Mall Pte Ltd.

The proposed development will comprise up to 875 residential units and 135,625 sq ft of commercial space, alongside a bus interchange, retail facilities, and a hawker centre.

Gamuda said the project is expected to contribute positively to earnings and shareholder value, with completion targeted within 84 months.

data.title: Singapore’s private banking industry seeks to strengthen philanthropy advisory
data.description:
They want to enhance advisors’ capabilities related to making philanthropic decisions.

The Wealth Management Institute (WMI) has partnered with the Private Banking Industry Group (PBIG) to advise capabilities and explore ways to elevate Singapore’s position as a hub for effective philanthropy.

The new phase of collaboration focuses on enhancing the capabilities of client-facing advisors who are the first point of contact for principals, wealth owners, and their families.

The goal is to better meet the growing demand for values-based philanthropy advisory that resonates with the next generation—who are often more attuned to social and environmental causes, the WMI and PBIG said in a press release.

Since 2022, WMI’s philanthropy programs have achieved about 600 enrolments. These programmes aim to equip advisors in guiding clients towards making more effective philanthropic decisions.

New programmes have also been planned, including a Certificate in Philanthropy and Social Impact for Greater China Markets to be launched in November 2025.

data.title: Aman to open first Singapore property at The Skywaters
data.description:
It will include a hotel and a limited number of branded residences.

Luxury hospitality brand Aman will open its first property in Singapore at The Skywaters, set to be the city’s tallest skyscraper.

The project will include a hotel, a limited number of branded residences, and an exclusive private members’ club.

Developed by a consortium led by Perennial Holdings, with design by Skidmore, Owings & Merrill, The Skywaters is located at the intersection of Singapore’s Central Business District, Marina Bay, and the future Greater Southern Waterfront.

Aman Singapore will feature interiors by Kerry Hill Architects. The property will include a floor of large hotel suites with views of the city and the South China Sea, an Aman Club, and a collection of residences ranging from one to five bedrooms.

Facilities will be reserved for residents, members, and guests, and will include an Aman Spa spanning an entire floor, an infinity pool, and several dining venues featuring global and signature Aman concepts.

The branded residences will provide full access to Aman services and amenities, offering a private retreat above Singapore’s cityscape.

data.title: Nintendo sets up Singapore subsidiary to drive SEA growth
data.description:
It has a capital stock of $8m.

Nintendo Co., Ltd. has established Nintendo Singapore Pte. Ltd. on 26 September 2025, as part of its expansion in Southeast Asia.

The new subsidiary, fully owned by Nintendo, has a capital stock of $8m and will focus on accelerating the company’s regional business.

The office will be led by Managing Director Takahiro Miura.

Nintendo also said it is considering creating a similar entity in Thailand.

data.title: mm2 Asia seeks 90-day extension for FY2025 AGM
data.description:
The delay is due to pending audit work.

mm2 Asia Ltd. has applied to the Singapore Exchange Regulation (SGX RegCo) for a further 90-day extension to meet its FY2025 reporting deadlines.

The company is requesting approval to hold its annual general meeting (AGM) by 27 January 2026 instead of 29 October 2025, to issue its annual report by 12 January 2026 instead of 14 October 2025, and to release its sustainability report by 12 January 2026 instead of 14 October 2025.

mm2 Asia said the delay is due to pending audit work. The auditors have agreed to start once outstanding invoices are progressively repaid.

In addition, the liquidation of subsidiaries Cathay Cineplexes Pte. Ltd., MM2 Star Screen Sdn. Bhd., and mm2 Screen Management Sdn. Bhd. has created complex accounting issues, including impairment reviews and provision recognition.

The company also applied to the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority (ACRA) for a 60-day extension to hold its AGM by 28 December 2025 and file its annual return by 28 January 2026.

It plans to seek another 30-day extension to push these deadlines to 27 January 2026 and 27 February 2026, respectively

data.title: Hong Leong unveils 462-unit Penrith development in Queenstown
data.description:
Prices for two-bedroom units start from $1.495m, or $2,437 per square foot.

Hong Leong Holdings, along with Hong Realty and GuocoLand, have launched Penrith, a 462-unit residential project in Queenstown.

The 99-year leasehold development spans 102,497 sq ft and comprises two 40-storey towers and features a mix of two- to four-bedroom units.

Prices for two-bedroom units start from $1.495m, or $2,437 per square foot.

Public previews will begin on 3 October, with sales to start on 18 October.

It is the first private residential launch in Queenstown since 2018, said Hong Leong.

data.title: Gov’t to stop use of Child Dev’t Accounts at optical shops, retail pharmacies
data.description:
This decision followed audit findings that showed a higher risk of misuse at these outlets.

The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) will discontinue the use of Child Development Account (CDA) money at optical shops and retail pharmacies from January 2026.

This decision followed audit findings that showed a higher risk of misuse at these outlets.

The Auditor-General’s Office (AGO) flagged poor record keeping and unusual spending patterns, including multiple withdrawals within short periods and purchases that did not appear to benefit the child or their siblings.

MSF’s follow-up investigations also found cases where CDA money was used for adults’ purchases, as well as instances where shops allowed parents to withdraw cash from the accounts—something not permitted under the scheme.

The change will not affect pharmacy services in healthcare institutions, such as hospitals and polyclinics, where CDA funds can still be used for prescribed medications and consultation fees.

The accounts can also continue to be used at Baby Bonus-approved institutions, such as GP clinics and polyclinics.

The CDA is a special co-savings account with government co-matching and can be used for approved educational and healthcare expenses for the child or their siblings.

Separately, since May 2025, CDA usage has been expanded to cover incidental charges and enrichment programmes at preschools.

data.title: Nio Inc. delivers 34,749 vehicles in September
data.description:
For Q3, it delivered 87,071 vehicles.

NIO Inc. delivered 34,749 vehicles in September, up 64.1% year-over-year and a new monthly record.

The total included 13,728 vehicles from its premium NIO brand, 15,246 from its family-oriented ONVO brand, and 5,775 from its FIREFLY small high-end EV brand.

For the third quarter, NIO delivered 87,071 vehicles, up 40.8% from the same period last year and setting a new quarterly record.

Cumulative deliveries as of 30 September 2025, reached 872,785 vehicles.

On September 20, the company officially launched its flagship All-New ES8, a premium three-row electric SUV, with customer deliveries starting shortly after.

Built on NIO’s decade of technological development, the All-New ES8 showcases NIO’s latest advancements in smart EV technology and aims to lead the large three-row SUV segment into the battery-electric era.

data.title: Yangzijiang leads consortium to acquire 23% stake in Ningbo Shanshan for restructuring
data.description:
About 223.3m shares bought at $2.07.

Yangzijiang Financial announced that its subsidiary, Jiangsu New Yangzi Commerce & Trading Co., Ltd, together with Xiamen TCL Industrial Investment Co., Ltd and China Orient Asset Management Co., Ltd (collectively, the Consortium), has signed an agreement to acquire a 23.36% effective voting stake in Ningbo Shanshan Co. Ltd. to lead the company’s restructuring.

In Phase 1, the Consortium set up an investment platform to buy 223.3 million Shanshan Co. shares (9.93% of total) from Shanshan Group Ltd and its subsidiary at $2.07 (RMB 11.44) per share).

Yangzijiang Financial will contribute $184.5m (RMB 1.02b) for a 40% stake in the platform. Xiamen TCL will acquire an additional 43.7 million shares (1.94%), with voting rights entrusted to the platform.

In Phase 2, a limited partnership with China Orient Asset Management will acquire 20 million shares (0.89%), with voting rights also entrusted to the platform. Voting rights of remaining shares held by the Sellers will similarly be entrusted.

Once complete, the Consortium will control 23.36% of Shanshan Co., with Yangzijiang Financial exercising voting rights as platform General Partner.

The investment represents 4.79% of Yangzijiang Financial’s net assets and 4.51% of its market capitalisation.

Completion is subject to creditors’ and court approval, and funding obligations must be met.

Founded in 1992, Shanshan Co. produces lithium battery anode materials and polarizers, serving the new energy, battery, and EV sectors.

The Consortium expects the investment to stabilise Shanshan Co.’s ownership, support long-term growth, and leverage operational expertise and industry networks.
